name: Algebraic condition for group commutators
statement_text:
A member α of G1 is a member of D(ξ) if

                               α(tu) + α(ut) = α(t)α(u),                            (388d)

whenever t, u ∈ T and r(t) ≤ ξ.
statement_latex:
\begin{definition}
A member $\alpha$ of $G_1$ is a member of $D(\xi)$ if
\[
\alpha(tu) + \alpha(ut) = \alpha(t)\alpha(u),
\]
whenever $t, u \in T$ and $r(t) \leq \xi$.
\end{definition}
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
Let $T$ be the set of rooted trees and $r(t)$ the order (number of vertices) of $t\in T$. Let $G_1$ be the group of elementary weight functions $\alpha$ corresponding to Runge--Kutta methods. For a parameter $\xi\in\mathbb{Z}$, define the subset $D(\xi)\subset G_1$ by the condition: for all $t,u\in T$,
\[
\alpha(t)\alpha(u)=\alpha(t\sqcup u)
\]
whenever $r(t)+r(u)\le\xi$.

variables.name: α
variables.definition: elementary weight function corresponding to a Runge–Kutta method, a member of G1

variables.name: G1
variables.definition: group of elementary weight functions for Runge–Kutta methods

variables.name: r(t)
variables.definition: order (or number of vertices) of a tree t

variables.name: ξ
variables.definition: parameter (integer) defining the condition, likely related to order

preamble: The context involves algebraic structures (groups and subgroups) based on elementary weight functions α of Runge–Kutta methods, with trees t, u ∈ T and order function r(t). The definition characterizes a subset D(ξ) of G1 via a relation on products of trees.
